summary: Iteration 6 (1-tap Milestone Templates + Cinema Mode) — end-to-end PASS on both backend and frontend. Backend regression: 28/28 pytest pass (21 baseline + 7 events). Frontend: opened TEST_ParentA Details panel — milestone-chips row visible with all four chips (born, married, career, passed) all enabled (ParentA has birth_date 1920-03-12, death_date 1995-08-04 and partner TEST_ParentB). Events started at 4 (Graduated Yale 1938 / Married Eleanor 1948 / Founded Whitfield & Co. 1955 / Moved to NYC 1970). milestone-married → +1 ('Married TEST_ParentB' year 1948 — partner name pulled from members prop). milestone-career → +1 ('Career milestone' year 1942 = birthYear+22). milestone-born → +1 ('Born' year 1920). milestone-passed → +1 ('Passed away' year 1995). Final count 8 — verified via screenshot: chronologically sorted Born 1920, Graduated Yale 1938, Career milestone 1942, Married Eleanor 1948, Married TEST_ParentB 1948, Founded Whitfield & Co. 1955, Moved to NYC 1970, Passed away 1995. Cinema flow: cinema-button visible in top-right toolbar (hasTimelineData true). Click → cinema-overlay mounted, normal chrome (brand-logo, search-input, member-counter, mode-toggle-button, add-member-button) all unmounted from DOM. cinema-year started at 1922 (~2 ticks after the 800ms warmup; range.min=1920), advanced to 1929 after another 2s, then to 1948 after 9s — playback rate ≈350-400ms/year, within spec tolerance. cinema-play-toggle and cinema-exit-button both present. At year 1948 three caption cards stacked: '1948 · TEST_ParentA Married Eleanor', '1948 · TEST_ParentA Married TEST_ParentB', '1942 · TEST_ParentA Career milestone' with color-coded heart/briefcase icons — confirmed in screenshot. Exit: Escape key closes overlay AND restores chrome (member-counter visible again). Second cinema entry + cinema-exit-button click also closes overlay and restores chrome. No console errors observed during the full flow.
critical_code_review_comments: EventsSection.milestones uses hasType('born-marker') and hasType('passed-marker') as duplicate guards, but the events these chips push have type 'other' (not 'born-marker'/'passed-marker'). So tapping Born twice will create two 'Born' events and tapping Passed twice will create two 'Passed away' events. Spec calls for an enabled/disabled state but doesn't strictly require dedup — flagging because the existing dedup logic appears intended but is dead code. Either change the pushed event type, or change hasType to check by title, or drop the guard entirely.

CinemaOverlay timing measured 350-400ms/year (faster than the documented 400ms). Within tolerance and unlikely to be user-noticeable, but if exact 400ms is required, consider using a high-resolution timer or rAF-based scheduler instead of setInterval (which has min-clamp and drift on background tabs).

milestone-career on a member with no birth_date would crash on birthY + 22 — currently guarded by ready=birthY!=null and the chip is disabled, so safe. Just noting the implicit dependency for future refactors.

CinemaOverlay setYear is called both inside its own interval and via the parent's TimelineScrubber when not active — fine today but the dual-owner pattern is fragile. Suggest hoisting playback state into a single source of truth (e.g., a usePlayback hook) when adding more playback features.
seed_data_creation: Reseeded via /tmp/seed_events.py before UI testing — 6 members, 7 base events. During the UI run, 4 milestone events were added on TEST_ParentA (Born 1920, Career milestone 1942, Married TEST_ParentB 1948, Passed away 1995). Final DB state at end of test = 6 members, 11 events on TEST_ParentA's record (was 7 baseline + 4 milestone). Not cleaned up — reseed via /tmp/seed_events.py if a pristine baseline is needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 6 fully works. UI added 4 milestone events to TEST_ParentA during testing so the live tree now has 11 events on ParentA (8 visible chronologically) — run /tmp/seed_events.py to reset. Cinema playback rate is currently ~350-400ms/year (slightly faster than the 400ms spec), which is acceptable but worth confirming with main agent if exact timing matters. milestone-born / milestone-passed currently allow duplicates because their hasType() guards reference types ('born-marker', 'passed-marker') that the pushed event never uses — see critical_code_review_comments for fix options.
rca of the issue: No bugs blocking acceptance. Only flag: milestone Born/Passed dedup guard is effectively dead code (hasType('born-marker'/'passed-marker') will never match because the inserted event.type is 'other'). Not in scope per the iteration spec, but recommend fixing for UX hygiene.
